title: Custom Computer Programming Services

alternatives.name: Customer-specific application development firms
inclusion_criteria: More of the firm's service revenue is attributable to designing and developing bespoke software applications for particular customers than to any other enumerated archetype.
The firm owns delivery of application code or a defined development workstream, whether contracted as a project, dedicated team, or time-and-materials engagement.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: Packaged-software customization or integration is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Website or database specialty work is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software support is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The establishment is primarily a software publisher, hosting provider, or computer-systems designer excluded from NAICS 541511 by the Census definition.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 target-band firms multiplied by a primary-equivalent share of 42.297%: the 2022 Economic Census reports that 61.3% of 541511 establishments provide custom software application design and development, and that this line supplies 69% of revenue at establishments reporting it. Multiplying prevalence by within-provider revenue share avoids counting every incidental service line as a whole firm. The 1,900-3,000 range allows for the product estimate's sampling variation, multiple service lines per establishment, firm-versus-establishment differences, and reweighting from the all-size Census population to the target band.

alternatives.name: Packaged-software customization and integration firms
inclusion_criteria: More of the firm's service revenue is attributable to customizing or integrating packaged software for customers than to any other enumerated archetype.
Customer work centers on configuring, extending, connecting, migrating, or implementing third-party or packaged applications.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: Bespoke application development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Website or database specialty work is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software support is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The firm primarily publishes its own software or primarily performs systems design without custom programming.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 multiplied by a 30.498% primary-equivalent share: 39.1% establishment prevalence for customization and integration of packaged software times a 78% within-provider revenue share. Its range is intentionally broad because packaged integration and bespoke development commonly coexist, the Census product rows are not mutually exclusive, and the target-band mix may differ from the all-size industry mix. Its high endpoint overlaps the selected archetype's low endpoint, so it remains a plausible largest-count alternative.

alternatives.name: Website and database development specialists
inclusion_criteria: Website design and development or database design and development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The firm delivers customer-specific programming rather than primarily selling hosting, advertising, graphic design, or a published software product.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: General bespoke application development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Packaged-software customization or integration is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software support is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Hosting, advertising, graphic design, or software publishing is the primary business.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 multiplied by 12.434%, combining non-overlapping primary-equivalent allocations for website services (30.8% prevalence times 31% within-provider revenue share) and database services (22.2% times 13%). The wide range reflects possible overlap between those two product rows, the distinction between a specialty firm and an incidental service line, and target-band reweighting.

alternatives.name: Programming capacity, technical consulting, and support-led firms
inclusion_criteria: Temporary staffing, IT technical consulting, or software-related technical support is the firm's largest service-revenue category while the establishment remains classified in NAICS 541511.
The customer primarily buys developer capacity, technical advice, or ongoing software support rather than vendor-owned delivery of a bespoke application or packaged-software implementation.
The firm is estimated to fall in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA target band represented by the pinned dataset.
exclusion_criteria: Bespoke application development is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Packaged-software customization or integration is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
Website or database specialty work is the firm's largest service-revenue category.
The establishment is primarily an employment service, management consultancy, or managed IT provider classified outside NAICS 541511.
alternatives.rationale: The base is 5,767 multiplied by an 8.15% primary-equivalent share: temporary staffing (2.5% prevalence times 35% within-provider revenue share), IT technical consulting (11.6% times 25%), and IT technical support (17.5% times 25%). The range is wide because these rows may overlap, may be incidental at programming firms, and may include business models near other NAICS boundaries. The staff-augmentation directory is used only to confirm that the engagement model exists, not to estimate its count.

residual.name: Mixed, other, and unclassifiable 541511 target-band firms
residual.rationale: The base is the 6.621% remainder after allocating the four primary-equivalent archetypes against the 5,767-firm band estimate. It includes firms without a dominant enumerated line, firms led by smaller Census product categories, and classification ambiguity. The endpoints allow for overlap and nonresponse in the product-line bridge.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition and cross-references for 541511 Custom Computer Programming Services
2022 Economic Census data release and access page
2022 Economic Census Industry by Products Statistics archive
Clutch custom software development company directory and service taxonomy
Clutch U.S. IT staff augmentation company directory and service taxonomy

target_archetype.selection_basis: Stable archetype IDs were fixed before any economics research. The pinned dataset estimates 5,767 target-band firms. For each Census product line, establishment prevalence was multiplied by that line's share of revenue at reporting establishments to form a primary-equivalent firm share; this converts overlapping service-line presence into a mutually exclusive principal-activity proxy. The resulting base counts are 2,439 custom-application firms, 1,759 packaged-integration firms, 717 web/database specialists, and 470 capacity/consulting/support-led firms, plus a 382-firm residual. Custom application development therefore wins the largest-base rule. Its selected-archetype coverage is 42.297% at base and approximately 32.9%-52.0% using its 1,900-3,000 count range over the 5,767 denominator. Selection uncertainty is true because the selected range overlaps the packaged-integration range; the record remains solely about the selected archetype and makes no claim to represent all of NAICS 541511.

value_basis.scope: Source-free, class-neutral cash-cost structure for target-band customer-specific application development firms; the bounds are widened to remain usable if firms near the boundary are reclassified as packaged-software integrators.

labor_contractor_cash_cost_share.rationale: This is a deliberately source-free bounded judgment that labor and contractors are the dominant but not exclusive cash cost of a mid-market custom programming firm. The range accommodates differences in onshore/offshore delivery, employee-versus-contractor mix, sales intensity, software and cloud spend, facilities, and reclassification toward packaged-software integration.
caveats: The pinned dataset's broad labor share and 5415 occupational mix are not used as deterministic target-archetype evidence.
The range is an assumption, not an empirical confidence interval, and forces readiness to UNPROVEN.
No later source may be attached to this frozen assumption without outcome-independent change control.

roles.title: Software engineering and architecture
roles.rationale: Frozen assumption for coding, technical design, code review, and engineering leadership in bespoke application delivery.

roles.title: Quality assurance and testing
roles.rationale: Frozen assumption for test design, execution, defect triage, release checks, and quality ownership.

roles.title: Business analysis, product translation, and delivery management
roles.rationale: Frozen assumption for requirements discovery, customer translation, project management, and delivery coordination.

roles.title: Integration, platform, data, and delivery operations
roles.rationale: Frozen assumption for system integration, environments, deployment, platform operations, and data work; retained to span the overlapping packaged-integration alternative.

roles.title: Sales, solutions, and account management
roles.rationale: Frozen assumption for business development, scoping support, proposals, contracting, and account stewardship.

roles.title: Corporate leadership and administration
roles.rationale: Frozen assumption for executive, finance, people, legal, security, and general administrative labor.

value_basis.rationale: A bounded assumption is preferable to treating the broader 5415 occupational mix or all-size NAICS labor ratio as target-specific. The six roles are mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive for labor and contractor cash cost, and sum to 1.00. The membership and weights are frozen before any implementation, pricing, or valuation research.
caveats: This basis supplies no evidence and cannot support readiness above UNPROVEN.
The selected and packaged-integration archetypes have overlapping count ranges; the 0.50-0.90 labor-cost interval is intentionally wider than a single-model estimate.
Role weights are point allocations required by the schema, while uncertainty is carried in the aggregate labor-and-contractor cash-cost range.
The record applies only to the selected customer-specific application development archetype.

source_hints.source: Target-archetype operator interviews, role-level workflow studies, implementation records, and customer delivery artifacts
source_hints.why: Needed to establish which frozen roles and tasks an evaluated workflow intervention can affect, the adoption schedule, required controls, and realized rather than demonstrated performance.

source_hints.source: Customer contracts, statements of work, pricing schedules, renewal records, win/loss data, and procurement interviews from target-band custom application firms
source_hints.why: Needed to distinguish fixed-price, time-and-materials, dedicated-team, and retainer economics and to test whether any delivery cost change is retained, passed through, or competed away.

source_hints.source: Lower-middle-market software-services transaction databases, broker listings, lender deal books, and ownership records filtered to the selected archetype and EBITDA band
source_hints.why: Needed to estimate how many qualifying firms are actually offered for sale and separately owned rather than counting all operating firms as purchasable targets.

source_hints.source: Closed-transaction multiples, quality-of-earnings reports, lender underwriting, and downside exit evidence for custom application development firms
source_hints.why: Needed to support entry and downside exit multiples and test sensitivity to customer concentration, utilization, contractor reliance, working capital, and recurring-revenue mix.

source_hints.source: Customer procurement standards, professional-liability and security requirements, contract accountability clauses, competitive entry data, and operator interviews
source_hints.why: Needed to determine whether an independent target-band operator retains a defensible delivery, trust, and accountability role through year five under the researched scenarios.

implementation: For each frozen role, which recurring tasks can the evaluated intervention technically affect in this archetype, and what fraction of role cash cost do those tasks represent?
What adoption, integration, security, review, retraining, rework, and customer-acceptance steps are required in years one through five?
What target-archetype evidence distinguishes demonstrated capability from realized production use and sustained cash-cost removal?
commercial_retention: How are projects priced across fixed-price, time-and-materials, dedicated-team, and support arrangements in the selected archetype?
Who contractually captures delivery efficiencies, and how do rebids, rate cards, scope expansion, change orders, and competition affect retention over five years?
Do customer concentration, subcontracting, partner-platform dependence, or procurement requirements materially change the supported capture range?
sale_availability: How many independently owned selected-archetype firms in the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band are offered for sale in a typical year?
How should broker coverage, unannounced transactions, add-on acquisitions, founder readiness, and duplicate listings be bridged to a five-year purchasable-target count?
Does the count remain bounded after excluding packaged-integration, staffing-led, web/database-specialist, publisher, and systems-design firms?
valuation_robustness: What entry multiples are observed for selected-archetype firms in the target band, with explicit bridges for date, size, geography, and business model?
What downside exit multiple is supported if utilization, growth, customer concentration, recurring-revenue mix, or competitive intensity deteriorates?
How sensitive are purchase economics to earnouts, working-capital adjustments, contractor normalization, founder replacement, and capitalized development practices?
operator_survival: Which customer-facing accountability, domain discovery, architecture, security, integration, and change-management functions must remain with a viable operator at year five?
Could customers, software platforms, independent specialists, staffing vendors, or new entrants bypass the selected operator, and on what supported timetable?
What evidence supports a bounded year-five survival probability for target-band customer-specific application development firms rather than the whole NAICS?

special_notes: This is a pre-economics, class-neutral specification; archetype IDs, base counts, selection, and assumption roles were fixed without consulting expected outcomes.

The 5,767-firm band denominator is LOW-quality in the normalized dataset because it is inferred from employment-size classes and a broad margin proxy; downstream readiness must therefore remain UNPROVEN unless the methodology explicitly permits otherwise.

Selection uncertainty is true because the custom-application and packaged-integration count ranges overlap. The single record remains tied to custom application development and must not be presented as the score of NAICS 541511.

The Economic Census product table reports overlapping product-line presence across all establishment sizes. Multiplying prevalence by within-provider line share is a transparent primary-equivalent proxy, not a direct count of target-band firms.
The assumption value basis has no sources, uses evidence state ASSUMPTION and quality NONE, and independently forces readiness to UNPROVEN.

Do not replace the frozen assumption basis with the normalized dataset's broader 5415 role mix or all-NAICS labor share; either would require an explicit target-specific bridge and outcome-independent change control.
